description_long: This is a weathered, rectangular metal street cabinet with a flat top and vertical sides, commonly found in urban environments. The surface is predominantly light gray, with significant areas of peeling paint and corrosion, especially on the front and sides, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. The front panel features a handle and a visible lock, as well as a large patch of worn paint and a prominent piece of street art depicting a person, along with several small stickers. The cabinet has a hinged door on the front, with the hinges visible on the right edge. There are no visible ventilation grilles or cable entry points on the exterior. The overall appearance suggests it is used to house electrical or communication equipment and is designed to be tamper-resistant.
description: A weathered, light gray metal street cabinet with peeling paint, a front door with a lock and handle, and street art on the front panel.
description_view_0: The left side of the cabinet, showing extensive peeling paint and corrosion, with no visible handles or markings.
description_view_1: The front view, featuring a handle, lock, large patch of worn paint, street art, and stickers; hinges are visible on the right.
description_view_2: The right side, similar to the left, with peeling paint and corrosion but no handles or markings.
description_view_3: The back panel, showing the largest area of peeling paint and corrosion, with no handles, locks, or markings.
